title: Surface Registry
description: Route-as-projection declarations for the web-dashboard platform surface. Each route is a declared projection of the canonical contract: it names its pillar, the projection(s) it consumes, granularity, transport, engine, and lifecycle. A surface may omit contract capability; it may never invent structure without a canonical source. CI asserts App.tsx route set == registry route set (every live route registered, honestly classified — deprecating/dev-only routes stay in the registry, not omitted) and that no pillar imports across the engine boundary.

path.description: Route path as registered in App.tsx.

pillar.description: The pillar or platform surface this route mounts.

engine.description: Rendering engine. r3f and speckle must never import each other's code (engine isolation, CI-asserted).

projection.description: Canonical projection per PHS SS1. speckle-stream = Viewer's independent surface. none = platform CRUD/admin surfaces outside the voxel/BOX canonical contract.

source.description: The endpoint or data source, e.g. /api/uro/voxel-groups/spatial, /ws/voxel-stream, Speckle API.

projections.description: One or more declared projections this route consumes. A route consuming both group- and voxel-level data (Coordination) declares both.

granularity.description: Data granularity. Coordination = both (group LOD-0 overview + voxel LOD-1 detail). Viewer = element.

lifecycle.description: active = current platform surface. deprecating = scheduled for removal, see retiresAt. dev-only = debug/dev tooling, not a production nav path, no removal implied.

retiresAt.description: The phase or deliverable that removes this route. Required in practice for lifecycle=deprecating; omitted for active/dev-only.

rails.description: Surfaces that are rails, not routes (omnipresent, project-scoped). SEPPA is a rail, not a route.
